title: Economics telemetry common types
description: Shared primitives for the economics telemetry contract (funnel events, run economics, receipts). Reference these definitions instead of re-declaring them per schema, to stop cross-schema drift. Every monetary and count primitive here is typed, nonnegative where the quantity cannot be negative, and bounded above, so a malformed or hostile payload is rejected by the contract rather than by downstream arithmetic.

ContractId.description: Stable identifier of this contract family. A payload that does not name this contract is not economics telemetry and is rejected.

ContractVersion.description: Stable version of the economics telemetry contract. Ingestion rejects unknown versions rather than best-effort parsing them; a breaking change ships a new const value.

UtcTimestamp.description: RFC-3339 timestamp in UTC, emitted with a trailing Z. The pattern enforces the stated form rather than describing it: `format: date-time` alone also admits a local offset (2026-07-17T13:00:00+03:00), and an offset-bearing timestamp that is read as UTC silently moves an event by hours — which reorders a funnel, moves a metering window, and backdates an attestation. This is a new contract with no emitters to break, so the honest choice is to enforce the form the prose promises; an emitter with a local clock normalizes to UTC before it emits, which it must do anyway to be comparable.

Identifier.description: Opaque caller-generated identifier. Carries no raw prompt, example, or customer content.

IdempotencyKey.description: Caller-generated idempotency key, unique per submitted batch. Mirrors the Idempotency-Key header convention used by the Planner V2 routes.

RunId.description: Experiment run identifier, aligned with the platform-wide RunId shape.

Sha256Hex.description: Lowercase hex SHA-256 digest. Used for dataset/holdout/config/example identity so evidence can be re-checked without shipping the underlying content.

UsdAmount.description: A nonnegative United States dollar amount. Bounded above at $100,000,000 so no unbounded or non-finite value enters budget arithmetic (JSON has no Infinity/NaN literal; the bound closes the remaining overflow case).

SignedUsdAmount.description: A United States dollar amount that may legitimately be negative (a delta, a loss, a net value). Bounded on both sides.

Count.description: A nonnegative bounded count (tokens, calls, examples, interventions).

DurationMs.description: A nonnegative duration in milliseconds, bounded at 24 hours.

Minutes.description: A nonnegative duration in minutes, bounded so reviewer-time claims cannot be inflated without bound.

ShortLabel.description: A bounded label (metric name, model id, version string). Not free-form content.

Provenance.description: How a characterization field's value was obtained. 'asked' = the end user answered it; 'inferred' = the coding agent derived it from artifacts and MUST carry evidence provenance (see EvidenceStatus); 'defaulted' = neither, the platform default was used.

SharingOutcome.description: What the client's sharing policy did with this field. 'withheld_by_policy' means the value never left the client machine; the telemetry records the withholding, not the value.

EvidenceStatus.description: Whether the evidence pointer backing an inferred value was shared. An inferred value always HAS evidence on the client; the client's sharing policy decides whether the pointer egresses. 'withheld_by_policy' asserts the evidence exists and was deliberately kept local.

EvidencePointer.description: One-line human-readable justification for an inferred value (e.g. 'traces show ~3.1k runs/day over 14 days'). Derived from the client's own traces/artifacts, so it is treated as user content: redact in logs and exports, and never emit it when the sharing policy withheld the evidence.
EvidencePointer.x-traigent-note: Present only when evidence_status = provided. The contract forbids this field when evidence_status = withheld_by_policy, so a policy-withheld pointer cannot egress through this field.

Archetype.description: Agent archetype driving budget floors/caps and promotion sentinels. 'undetermined' is an honest value: it records that classification failed rather than forcing a wrong archetype.

MeasurementUnit.description: Unit of a measured effect or metric change. Closed so a consumer never has to guess what a delta means.

lower.description: Lower bound of the interval. Ordering (lower <= estimate <= upper) is a backend obligation, reported as interval_bounds_inconsistent; JSON Schema cannot compare two sibling values. Not a cosmetic check: the economics model leads with the lower bound, so an inverted interval turns an unfavourable result into a favourable claim.

level.description: Interval coverage level (e.g. 0.95). Required: an interval without its level cannot be read as a lower bound.

ConfidenceInterval.description: An effect estimate with an explicit interval and level. Required wherever the economics model leads with a lower bound, so a point estimate can never be mistaken for evidence.

PriceSource.description: Where a model price came from. Closed: an agent-asserted price is not a price source.

ModelPrice.description: The price actually used to dollarize this run's token usage, with its source and as-of time, so a cost claim is reproducible after provider prices change.

Source.description: Identity of the emitting surface. Required on every batch so funnel counts can be attributed to a surface and version.
